nodes.label: AI Engineer
nodes.description: An engineer who develops AI models and integrates them into applications.

nodes.label: Feature Engineering
nodes.description: The process of using domain knowledge to select and transform raw data into features that better represent the underlying problem.

nodes.label: Neural Networks
nodes.description: Computational models inspired by the human brain, used in deep learning.

nodes.label: Overfitting
nodes.description: A modeling error that occurs when a machine learning model learns noise instead of the underlying pattern.

nodes.label: Gradient Boosting
nodes.description: An ensemble technique that builds models sequentially to correct errors from previous models.

nodes.label: Transfer Learning
nodes.description: A method where a pre-trained model is fine-tuned on a new task to improve performance.

nodes.label: Python
nodes.description: A high-level programming language widely used in data science, machine learning, and NLP.

nodes.label: XGBoost
nodes.description: An optimized gradient boosting library designed to be highly efficient, flexible, and portable.

nodes.label: Apache Airflow
nodes.description: An open-source tool for orchestrating complex data workflows.

nodes.label: SHAP
nodes.description: A tool for interpreting machine learning models by quantifying feature contributions.

nodes.label: Prometheus
nodes.description: An open-source monitoring and alerting toolkit widely used for monitoring machine learning models.

nodes.label: AI System Design
nodes.description: The process of designing AI systems to meet specific requirements.

nodes.label: Behavioral Interviews
nodes.description: Interviews that assess a candidate's past behavior in professional situations to predict future performance.

nodes.label: Statistics
nodes.description: The study of data collection, analysis, interpretation, presentation, and organization.

nodes.label: TensorFlow
nodes.description: An open-source machine learning framework developed by Google for building and training models.

nodes.label: PyTorch
nodes.description: An open-source machine learning library used for applications such as computer vision and natural language processing.

nodes.label: Logistic Regression
nodes.description: A statistical method for predicting binary classes.

nodes.label: Transformers
nodes.description: A deep learning model architecture that uses self-attention mechanisms to process sequential data, particularly in NLP.

nodes.label: MLOps Engineer
nodes.description: A professional responsible for managing the lifecycle of machine learning models, including deployment, monitoring, and maintenance.

nodes.label: ML System Design
nodes.description: The process of designing scalable machine learning systems to solve business problems.

nodes.label: AI Engineering Interview Questions
nodes.description: A collection of questions and answers to prepare for AI engineering interviews.

nodes.label: Project Deep Dive
nodes.description: An interview format where candidates present and discuss their past projects.

nodes.label: Data Modeling
nodes.description: The process of defining how data is structured and stored.

nodes.label: Communication
nodes.description: The ability to convey information effectively and efficiently.

nodes.label: Kubernetes
nodes.description: An open-source system for automating the deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.

nodes.label: LLM System Design
nodes.description: The process of designing large language models for specific applications.

nodes.label: Reinforcement Learning
nodes.description: A type of machine learning where an agent learns to make decisions by receiving rewards or penalties.

nodes.label: GRPO
nodes.description: Group Relative Policy Optimization, a reinforcement learning technique for fine-tuning LLMs.

nodes.label: Proximal Policy Optimization
nodes.description: An algorithm used in reinforcement learning to optimize policies.

nodes.label: Docker
nodes.description: A platform for developing, shipping, and running applications in containers.

nodes.label: Experimentation
nodes.description: The practice of testing hypotheses through controlled experiments to validate model performance.

nodes.label: Machine Learning
nodes.description: A field of artificial intelligence that uses statistical techniques to give computer systems the ability to learn from data.

nodes.label: Deep Learning
nodes.description: A subset of machine learning that uses neural networks with many layers to analyze various factors of data.

nodes.label: Natural Language Processing
nodes.description: A field of AI that enables computers to understand, interpret, and respond to human language.

nodes.label: Data Mining
nodes.description: The practice of examining large databases to generate new information.

nodes.label: Machine Learning Algorithms
nodes.description: Methods used to enable machines to learn from data and make predictions.

nodes.label: Optimization Techniques
nodes.description: Strategies used to improve the performance of machine learning models.

nodes.label: Keras
nodes.description: An open-source software library that provides a Python interface for neural networks.

nodes.label: CNNs
nodes.description: Convolutional Neural Networks, a class of deep learning algorithms used for image processing.

nodes.label: Gradient Descent
nodes.description: An optimization algorithm used to minimize the cost function in machine learning models.

nodes.label: Computer Vision Engineer
nodes.description: A professional who specializes in developing algorithms and systems that enable computers to interpret and understand visual data.

nodes.label: Computer Vision
nodes.description: A field of AI focused on enabling machines to interpret and understand visual information from the world.

nodes.label: Data Augmentation
nodes.description: A technique used to increase the diversity of training datasets by applying random transformations.

nodes.label: YOLO
nodes.description: A real-time object detection algorithm that treats detection as a single regression problem.

nodes.label: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)
nodes.description: A class of machine learning frameworks designed to generate new, synthetic instances of data that can pass as authentic.

nodes.label: Image Segmentation
nodes.description: The process of dividing an image into multiple segments to simplify its representation and make it more meaningful.

nodes.label: Model Evaluation
nodes.description: The process of assessing the performance of a machine learning model using various metrics.

nodes.label: OpenCV
nodes.description: An open-source computer vision and machine learning software library.

nodes.label: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN)
nodes.description: A deep learning algorithm primarily used for image processing and computer vision tasks.

nodes.label: Data Scientist
nodes.description: A professional who uses statistical methods and algorithms to analyze and interpret complex data.

nodes.label: Machine Learning Engineer
nodes.description: A professional who designs and implements machine learning models and systems.

nodes.label: ReLU
nodes.description: A non-linear activation function used in neural networks to introduce non-linearity.

nodes.label: Max Pooling
nodes.description: A down-sampling technique used in CNNs to reduce the dimensionality of feature maps.

nodes.label: Image Processing
nodes.description: The manipulation of an image to enhance its quality or extract useful information.

nodes.label: Feature Detection
nodes.description: The process of identifying and locating specific features within an image.

nodes.label: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)
nodes.description: A deep learning algorithm particularly effective for image processing tasks.

nodes.label: Vision Transformer
nodes.description: A model that applies transformer architecture to image processing tasks.

nodes.description: The task of identifying and classifying objects within an image.

nodes.label: SIFT (Scale-Invariant Feature Transform)
nodes.description: An algorithm used for detecting and describing local features in images.

nodes.label: Feature Extraction
nodes.description: The process of converting data into sets of features that can provide a more accurate understanding of that data.

nodes.label: Probability
nodes.description: A branch of mathematics dealing with the likelihood of different outcomes.

nodes.label: Linear Algebra
nodes.description: A branch of mathematics essential for understanding machine learning algorithms.

nodes.label: Object Detection
nodes.description: A computer vision task that identifies objects in an image and their locations.

nodes.label: Image Reconstruction
nodes.description: The process of generating a new image from processed or transformed data, often used in tasks like super-resolution and denoising.

nodes.label: Optical Character Recognition (OCR)
nodes.description: A technology used to convert different types of documents into editable and searchable data.

nodes.description: An open-source web application that allows the creation and sharing of documents containing live code, equations, visualizations, and narrative text.

nodes.description: A technique used to reduce the size of a neural network by removing unnecessary parameters.

nodes.label: Real-time Object Detection
nodes.description: The process of identifying and classifying objects in images or video streams in real-time.

nodes.description: An interactive experience where real-world environments are enhanced by computer-generated perceptual information.

nodes.label: Machine Learning Bias
nodes.description: The presence of systematic errors in machine learning models that can lead to unfair outcomes.

nodes.label: Image Classification
nodes.description: A foundational task in computer vision that involves assigning a label to an image based on its content.

nodes.label: ResNet
nodes.description: A deep learning model known for its residual learning framework, widely used in image classification.

nodes.label: VGG
nodes.description: A convolutional neural network model known for its depth and simplicity, used in image classification tasks.

nodes.label: SQL

nodes.description: An open-source relational database management system.

nodes.description: A component of Apache Spark that allows querying structured data using SQL.

nodes.label: Performance Tuning
nodes.description: The process of optimizing the performance of Spark applications.

nodes.label: Data Skew
nodes.description: A situation where some partitions have significantly more data than others.

nodes.description: A Spark component for processing live data streams.

nodes.label: Spark
nodes.description: An open-source distributed computing system used for big data processing.

nodes.label: SQL Optimization
nodes.description: Techniques to improve the performance of SQL queries.

nodes.label: Airflow
nodes.description: A platform to programmatically author, schedule, and monitor workflows.

nodes.description: Interview questions focused on the design of data systems for batch and streaming data.

nodes.description: A comprehensive cloud computing platform provided by Amazon.

nodes.description: Fundamentals of Python programming for building data pipelines.

nodes.description: A statistical method that uses sample data to evaluate a hypothesis about a population parameter.

nodes.description: A statistical test used to compare the means of two groups.

nodes.description: A measure of the probability that an observed difference could have occurred just by random chance.

nodes.description: A statistical theory that states that the distribution of sample means approaches a normal distribution as the sample size increases.

nodes.description: A statistical test used to determine if there is a significant association between categorical variables.

nodes.label: ANOVA
nodes.description: A statistical method used to compare the means of three or more samples.

nodes.description: A web-based platform for version control and collaboration on software projects.

nodes.description: A statistical process for estimating the relationships among variables.

nodes.description: A statistical method that applies probability to statistical problems.

nodes.label: Linear Regression
nodes.description: A fundamental statistical method used to model the relationship between a dependent variable and one or more independent variables.

nodes.label: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)

nodes.description: A platform for data science competitions and collaborative projects.

nodes.label: NLP Engineer
nodes.description: A professional who specializes in Natural Language Processing, focusing on enabling machines to understand and interpret human language.

nodes.description: The process of designing systems that can understand and generate human language.

nodes.label: Tokenization
nodes.description: The process of splitting text into smaller units, such as words or sentences, for analysis.

nodes.label: Embeddings
nodes.description: Dense vector representations of words that capture semantic meanings, crucial for various NLP applications.

nodes.label: Sentiment Analysis
nodes.description: The computational study of people's emotions, attitudes, and opinions from text data.

nodes.label: BERT
nodes.description: A transformer-based model designed for understanding the context of words in a sentence by reading text bidirectionally.

nodes.label: Word2Vec
nodes.description: A popular algorithm for creating word embeddings by predicting words from context.

nodes.label: Transformer
nodes.description: A deep learning model architecture that uses self-attention mechanisms to process sequential data.

nodes.label: Sequence-to-Sequence Models
nodes.description: Models that convert input sequences into output sequences, commonly used in NLP tasks.

nodes.label: Attention Function
nodes.description: A mechanism that determines the importance of different input elements when generating outputs.

nodes.label: Multi-head Attention
nodes.description: An extension of attention mechanisms that allows the model to focus on different parts of the input simultaneously.

nodes.label: ChatGPT
nodes.description: A conversational AI model developed by OpenAI that uses deep learning to generate human-like text.

nodes.label: Word Embeddings
nodes.description: Techniques used in NLP to represent words in a continuous vector space, capturing semantic meanings.

nodes.label: Self-Attention
nodes.description: A mechanism that allows models to weigh the importance of different tokens in a sequence when making predictions.

nodes.label: Multi-Head Attention
nodes.description: An extension of self-attention that allows the model to focus on different parts of the input sequence simultaneously.

nodes.label: Attention Mechanism
nodes.description: A technique in neural networks that allows models to focus on specific parts of the input sequence.

nodes.label: NLP
nodes.description: Natural Language Processing, a field of AI that focuses on the interaction between computers and human language.

nodes.label: GloVe

nodes.label: FastText
nodes.description: An extension of Word2Vec that represents words as bags of character n-grams.

nodes.label: TF-IDF

nodes.description: An open-source software library for advanced natural language processing in Python.

nodes.label: Machine Translation

nodes.label: Self Attention
nodes.description: An attention mechanism that allows a model to weigh the significance of different words in a sequence when processing them.

nodes.label: Transformers Library

links.description: Tokenization is closely related to embeddings as it prepares text for generating word representations.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Tokenization is a fundamental process related to Natural Language Processing.

links.label: USES
links.description: AutoTokenizer is a tool that automates the tokenization process in NLP tasks.

links.label: USES
links.description: Natural Language Processing utilizes BERT for understanding context in language.

links.label: REQUIRES
links.description: Masked Language Modeling is a training technique specifically used for BERT.

links.label: USES
links.description: BERT can be applied to Named Entity Recognition tasks to improve accuracy.

links.label: USES
links.description: Natural Language Processing uses Word2Vec for generating word embeddings.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: The Transformer architecture utilizes attention functions to process input sequences.

links.label: USES
links.description: Transformers employ multi-head attention to capture information from different representation subspaces.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Transformers are designed to address the limitations of traditional sequence-to-sequence models.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Multi-head attention is an extension of the attention function that enhances its capabilities.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Self Attention is a specific type of Attention Mechanism used in Transformers.

links.label: USES
links.description: Natural Language Processing employs GloVe for obtaining word vector representations.

links.label: USES
links.description: TF-IDF is used in Natural Language Processing for evaluating word importance.
